title: Smoke Hot Korean Pork Stew
description: A winey, glassy-jelly, cozy Korean dish featuring stout, smoke-hot, gluten-free-friendly ingredients, this stew is perfect for a cold winter night. It combines the rich flavors of pork, vegetables, and spices in a hearty, comforting meal.
instructions: Heat the sesame oil in a large Dutch oven over medium-high heat.
Add the pork and cook until browned on all sides, about 5 minutes.
Add the garlic and ginger and cook for 1 minute, until fragrant.
Add the gochujang, soy sauce, and brown sugar, and cook for 1 minute, until the sauce is bubbly.
Add the stout beer and bring to a boil, scraping up any browned bits from the bottom of the pot.
Add the potatoes and zucchini, and bring to a simmer.
Reduce the heat to low and cook, covered, until the pork is tender and the vegetables are cooked through, about 20 minutes.
Season with salt and black pepper to taste.
Serve hot, garnished with green onions and toasted sesame seeds if desired.
